content.title: Meet Deckyard
content.subheading: A presentation engine for humans and AI agents
content.byline: Decks as data, not as files

content.title: What it is
content.body:
Deckyard is a **self-hosted presentation engine**. A deck is a plain, versioned data document — an envelope, a list of slide types, and an array of slides.

Because the deck is data, a person can edit it in the browser and an agent can generate it through an API, and both produce exactly the same result.

content.title: What you get
content.subheading: The pieces that make it work

items.title: Decks as data
items.text: A portable JSON format you can diff and version

items.title: A typed slide system
items.text: Each slide type declares its own fields

items.title: Inline editing
items.text: Click any text on the canvas to change it

items.title: Themes
items.text: Swap the whole look without touching content

items.title: No framework, no bundler
items.text: Vanilla JS modules on client and server

items.title: Agent-friendly
items.text: The format is the API surface

content.title: One format, many surfaces
content.subheading: Where a single deck can be rendered
content.data:
Surface,Supported
Live present,1
Self-serve editor,1
Static export,1
Agent API,1
Embed / follow,1

content.title: Deckyard vs a slide file
content.leftBody:
- Opaque; hard to diff or review
- One app can open it well
- Automation means brittle scripting
- Styling is baked into every slide
content.rightBody:
- **Plain data** you can read and version
- Any renderer can consume it
- Agents generate it directly
- Theme is separate from content

content.title: Why decks-as-data
content.subheading: The idea the whole engine is built around

content.quote: The format is the product. Once the deck is just data, everything else — editing, exporting, generating — is a view over it.
I had an agent draft the whole deck, then cleaned it up by clicking on the slides. Same document the whole way through.

content.title: Try it here
content.body:
You're looking at a live editor. Click any headline or paragraph to change it, reorder the slides, swap the theme from the toolbar.

Nothing you do here is saved to anyone else — this is your sandbox.

content.title: Make something
content.body: Start from this deck, or clear it and build your own.
